working_title: Learning-Augmented Event-Triggered MPC for Supply-Chain Resilience under Nonstationary Disruptions: Exact Static Benchmarks and Full-DES Validation

confirmed: Program Q RecurrentPPO beats the complete 65536-calendar open-loop frontier in all three cells
Program Q RecurrentPPO is practically equivalent in canonical ReT to the strongest tested structured controller
Program Q worst-product-fill non-inferiority versus the structured controller is not established
the reduced exact benchmark is solved and horizon-3 MPC reaches its exact optimum
prospective: residual full-DES headroom beyond strengthened ReT-aligned MPC

physical_contract.event_tie_robustness: both plausible simultaneous-event orderings, frozen before confirmation

allowed: observable product inventory, WIP, pipeline and backlog
order ages and recent realized demand
causal history available at the decision time

U2_timing.estimand: V(best variable-review adaptive controller with four review rights) - V(best fixed-cadence adaptive controller with the same four review rights)
U2_timing.unconstrained_weekly_oracle_role: upper ceiling only; report V(variable-review oracle) - V(unconstrained weekly oracle) as non-positive regret, never as a superiority estimand

components: constrained scenario MPC over product mix and review time
calibrated confidence gate with robust feasible MPC fallback

claim_limit: If confirmed, claim best performance among the complete open-loop frontier and all preregistered tested adaptive families under matched information, resources, review rights and online computation. Never claim superiority over every mathematically possible dynamic policy or deployment safety.
failure_route: Preserve the failed gate and route the executed Program Q decomposition manuscript to submission without another rescue environment.
